page.content: In the pursuit of optimizing portfolio performance, the analysis of alternative investment returns within the Asia-Pacific region has garnered significant attention from institutional investors.

lines.text: In the pursuit of optimizing portfolio performance, the analysis of alternative investment returns within the Asia-Pacific region
has garnered significant attention from institutional investors.

page.content: This report provides a comprehensive examination of various alternative investment vehicles, highlighting their respective returns and associated risks through metrics such as the Sharpe ratio and tracking error. Notably, the table illustrates the performance of Private Equity, which has achieved a return of 12.3%, signifying its potential as a robust asset class in diversifying investment portfolios.

page.content: The tracking error for Hedge Funds is also noteworthy, recorded at 0.10%, which reflects the degree of deviation from benchmark returns and highlights the effectiveness of active management strategies in this sector. Additionally, the Sharpe ratios for different funds, including FundC and FundD, provide critical insights into risk-adjusted returns, enabling investors to gauge the relative merit of these investments.

page.content: Overall, this analysis aims to equip asset managers and institutional investors with the necessary data to make informed decisions in an increasingly complex investment landscape.
